mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS trends lower, discount rates and policy stress fall, lifting equity valuations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is also decelerating, because easing without renewed inflation reduces the chance of a hawkish reversal.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS falls over a 1-3 month window and CPIAUCSL is flat or lower, or if the signal's rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and demand, which raises recession risk and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should appear after a clear labor-market deterioration rather than on small month-to-month noise.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the average return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in RSAFS combined with stable or improving PAYEMS indicates broad demand expansion and better revenue expectations, which supports S&P 500 gains over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if sales growth is concentrated in a weak labor backdrop, because then revenue strength is less likely to persist.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60-day returns do not exceed negative returns in periods where RSAFS is rising and PAYEMS is also rising, or if the interaction term between sales and payroll growth is insignificant across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and supply-side momentum, which typically supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when output gains are not accompanied by a jump in CPIAUCSL, because inflationary supply stress can offset the benefit.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show positive average 20-day returns, or if the sign flips negative when CPIAUCSL accelerates only modestly rather than sharply.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST after prior weakness signals demand softening and a housing-led slowdown that typically weighs on equities over the next 60 trading days. The effect should be stronger when UNRATE is rising, because housing weakness then reflects economy-wide deterioration rather than temporary volatility.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns are not negative on average during periods of falling HOUST with rising UNRATE, or if the joint condition has no incremental predictive power beyond UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level above a restrictive threshold tightens financial conditions and lowers equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal is most active when CPIAUCSL is still elevated, because restrictive policy in an inflationary environment implies continued valuation pressure.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of episodes with FEDFUNDS above the threshold and CPIAUCSL above its recent trend, or if the conditional mean return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A stable or declining PAYEMS count indicates resilient payroll creation and supports earnings expectations, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when payroll growth is accompanied by rising UNRATE, because that combination implies job creation is not translating into labor-market tightness.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 20-day returns are not more frequent when PAYEMS is rising and UNRATE is flat or lower, or if the interaction effect becomes insignificant in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDPC1 growth above its recent pace signals stronger aggregate demand and should support S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days through higher revenue growth and lower recession odds. The effect is invalid if GDP growth is purely inventory-driven and does not coincide with improving RSAFS, because sales confirmation is needed for equity strength.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive on a quarterly basis
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP upturns without concurrent sales improvement fail to produce positive 60-day returns, or if the signal's out-of-sample hit rate falls below 55% over any 4-year window.
